title: Crumbly Middle Eastern Wild Mushroom Crockpot Delight
description: This herbal crumbly dish is a hearty and flavorful lazy-sunday meal featuring wild mushrooms that are pressure-cooked to perfection in an aromatic blend of herbs.

ingredients.name: Wild Mushrooms
ingredients.notes: Choose fresh wild mushrooms such as boletus or porcini

ingredients.name: Turmeric Powder
ingredients.notes: For color and to add depth of flavor

ingredients.name: Cumin Seeds
ingredients.notes: To enhance the dish's savory notes

ingredients.name: Coriander Seeds
ingredients.notes: For a touch of spice and freshness

instructions: Wash the wild mushrooms thoroughly and remove any dirt or debris. Clean them gently under running water.
Heat olive oil in a pressure cooker over medium heat. Add chopped onion, sauté until translucent (about 3 minutes).
Add garlic cloves if using, then stir in turmeric powder, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, and fresh herb bundle. Cook for another minute to release flavors.
Transfer the wild mushrooms into the pressure cooker along with their water from washing, pressing them down slightly to extract juices (if not pre-washed).
Pour enough water to cover all ingredients in the pressure cooker. Close lid and seal valve as per your pressure cooker's instructions for a high-pressure cooking time of about 10 minutes.

Once cooking is done, quick release the pressure manually by opening the valve or using an automatic method such as turning off heat, waiting until it naturally releases pressure (this could take up to 20-30 minutes), and then carefully lifting the lid. Allow the mixture to cool slightly before mashing or crumbing.
Serve with crusty bread or a side salad for a complete meal.
